sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: Entact Bio has reinforced its strategic positioning in induced-proximity therapeutics by appointing John G. Houston, Ph.D., to its Board of Directors, leveraging his pivotal role in advancing the first FDA-approved PROTAC drug, Veppanu (vepdegestrant), during his tenure at Arvinas. This leadership addition supports Entact’s proprietary Encompass™ platform, which utilizes ENTAC™ medicines to enhance beneficial protein function via deubiquitylases, distinguishing its approach from traditional protein degradation while capitalizing on the therapeutic potential demonstrated by PROTACs. While this development marks a significant milestone for proximity-inducing modalities, a comprehensive review of June 2026 pharmaceutical intelligence sources, including extensive STAT News publications, indicates no additional PROTAC-specific clinical trials, regulatory approvals, or corporate transactions were reported within that broader corpus. The available intelligence from those sources focused primarily on regulatory shifts such as FDA clinical trial pilot programs and CMS Medicare Advantage recalculations, rather than targeted protein degradation pipelines. Consequently, the current landscape reflects Entact Bio’s specific advancements in protein enhancement as the primary PROTAC-adjacent narrative, contrasting with a wider industry reporting environment dominated by vaccine licensing and policy reforms without explicit detail on small-molecule degrader candidates or clinical outcomes in this specific timeframe.

points.text: Entact Bio — ENTAC™ — Loss-of-function disease — Board Appointment of John G. Houston
Arvinas — Veppanu (vepdegestrant) — Not specified — First FDA-approved PROTAC drug
Entact Bio: Developing precision medicines to enhance beneficial protein function using Encompass™ platform and ENTAC™ medicines.
Arvinas: Advanced first FDA-approved PROTAC drug (Veppanu) under Houston's leadership.
ENTAC™ medicines: Leverage induced-proximity mechanism similar to PROTACs but enhance beneficial proteins via deubiquitylases (DUBs).
Veppanu (vepdegestrant): First FDA-approved type of proximity-inducing drugs called PROTACs.
